quiz.title: SAT Math Readiness Diagnostic Prototype
quiz.instructions: Original SAT Math-correlated readiness diagnostic prototype. Offline payload artifact only; do not treat as a live Canvas upload.

entry.title: ITEM-001
entry.item_body: If 3x + 5 = 20, what is the value of x?

feedback.neutral: Subtract 5 from both sides to get 3x = 15, then divide by 3 to get x = 5.

answer_feedback.ITEM-001-B: This subtracts 5 but does not divide by 3.
answer_feedback.ITEM-001-C: This adds 5 instead of subtracting 5 before dividing.
answer_feedback.ITEM-001-D: This uses the opposite sign after solving.

entry.title: ITEM-002
entry.item_body: The equation 2y - 4x = 10 represents a line. What is the slope of the line?

feedback.neutral: Rewrite as 2y = 4x + 10, then y = 2x + 5. The slope is 2.

answer_feedback.ITEM-002-B: This keeps the sign from -4x instead of solving for y.
answer_feedback.ITEM-002-C: This uses the coefficient of x before dividing by 2.
answer_feedback.ITEM-002-D: This identifies the y-intercept, not the slope.

entry.title: ITEM-003
entry.item_body: A train travels 144 miles in 3 hours at a constant speed. What is the speed, in miles per hour?

feedback.neutral: Divide miles by hours: 144 / 3 = 48 miles per hour.

answer_feedback.ITEM-003-A: This divides by 4 rather than by the 3 hours given.
answer_feedback.ITEM-003-C: This subtracts the time from the distance.
answer_feedback.ITEM-003-D: This multiplies distance by time instead of dividing.

properties.subdomain: Ratios, rates, proportional relationships, and units

entry.title: ITEM-004
entry.item_body: A triangle has base 14 units and height 9 units. What is its area, in square units?

feedback.neutral: Triangle area is (1/2)bh = (1/2)(14)(9) = 63.

answer_feedback.ITEM-004-A: This adds the base and height instead of using area.
answer_feedback.ITEM-004-C: This multiplies base by height but omits the factor of 1/2.
answer_feedback.ITEM-004-D: This squares the base instead of using both dimensions.

entry.title: ITEM-005
entry.item_body: Which inequality is equivalent to 5 - 2x > 13?

feedback.neutral: Subtract 5 to get -2x > 8. Dividing by -2 reverses the inequality, so x < -4.

answer_feedback.ITEM-005-A: This does not reverse the inequality when dividing by -2.
answer_feedback.ITEM-005-C: This misses both the negative value and the inequality reversal.
answer_feedback.ITEM-005-D: This reverses the inequality but loses the negative value.

properties.subdomain: Linear inequalities in one or two variables

entry.title: ITEM-006
entry.item_body: For x not equal to 3, which expression is equivalent to (x^2 - 9)/(x - 3)?

feedback.neutral: Factor x^2 - 9 as (x - 3)(x + 3). Cancel x - 3 to get x + 3.

answer_feedback.ITEM-006-A: This cancels the wrong factor after factoring the numerator.
answer_feedback.ITEM-006-C: This does not factor the difference of squares.

entry.title: ITEM-007
entry.item_body: If x + y = 11 and x - y = 3, what is the value of x?

feedback.neutral: Add the equations to get 2x = 14, so x = 7.

answer_feedback.ITEM-007-A: This is the value of y, not x.
answer_feedback.ITEM-007-C: This comes from subtracting equations incorrectly.
answer_feedback.ITEM-007-D: This adds the equations but does not divide by 2.

properties.subdomain: Systems of two linear equations in two variables

entry.title: ITEM-008
entry.item_body: If f(x) = x^2 - 4x, what is f(6)?

answer_feedback.ITEM-008-A: This computes (6 - 4)^2 instead of substituting 6 into both terms of x^2 - 4x.

entry.title: ITEM-009
entry.item_body: A price of $80 is increased by 15%. What is the new price?

feedback.neutral: The increase is 0.15(80) = 12, so the new price is 80 + 12 = 92.

answer_feedback.ITEM-009-A: This finds only the amount of increase.
answer_feedback.ITEM-009-B: This subtracts the increase instead of adding it.
answer_feedback.ITEM-009-D: This adds 15 dollars instead of 15 percent.

entry.title: ITEM-010
entry.item_body: The line y = 3x - 7 passes through the point (k, 8). What is the value of k?

feedback.neutral: Substitute y = 8: 8 = 3k - 7, so 15 = 3k and k = 5.

entry.title: ITEM-011
entry.item_body: What is the smaller solution to x^2 - 5x + 6 = 0?

feedback.neutral: Factor as (x - 2)(x - 3) = 0. The solutions are 2 and 3, so the smaller solution is 2.

answer_feedback.ITEM-011-A: This chooses a factor of 6 but does not check that the two factors add to 5.
answer_feedback.ITEM-011-C: This is a solution, but it is the larger solution.
answer_feedback.ITEM-011-D: This is the product of the two solutions, not a solution.

entry.title: ITEM-012
entry.item_body: A right triangle has legs of lengths 9 and 12. What is the length of the hypotenuse?

entry.title: ITEM-013
entry.item_body: A line passes through (2, 7) and (5, 19). What is the slope of the line?

answer_feedback.ITEM-013-A: This is the change in x, not the slope.
answer_feedback.ITEM-013-C: This divides the change in y by 2 instead of the change in x.
answer_feedback.ITEM-013-D: This is the change in y, not the slope.

entry.title: ITEM-014
entry.item_body: If h(x) = 2x^2 + 3x - 5, which expression is equivalent to h(x + 1) - h(x)?

feedback.neutral: h(x + 1) = 2(x + 1)^2 + 3(x + 1) - 5 = 2x^2 + 7x. Subtracting h(x) gives 4x + 5.

answer_feedback.ITEM-014-B: This expands h(x + 1) with an incorrect constant term.
answer_feedback.ITEM-014-C: This treats the change in 2x^2 as 2x and includes only the +3 from the linear term.
answer_feedback.ITEM-014-D: This computes h(x + 1) but does not subtract h(x).

entry.title: ITEM-015
entry.item_body: Six numbers have a mean of 18. When the largest number is removed, the mean of the remaining five numbers is 16. What is the largest number?

feedback.neutral: The six numbers have total 6*18 = 108. The remaining five numbers have total 5*16 = 80. The removed largest number is 108 - 80 = 28.

entry.title: ITEM-016
entry.item_body: For what value of a is a(2x - 3) = 10x - 15 true for all values of x?

feedback.neutral: Distribute to get 2ax - 3a. Matching 10x - 15 requires 2a = 10 and -3a = -15, so a = 5.

answer_feedback.ITEM-016-A: This matches the constant inside the parentheses, not the distributed expression.
answer_feedback.ITEM-016-C: This matches the coefficient of x before accounting for 2x.
answer_feedback.ITEM-016-D: This matches the constant term without considering the sign and factor.

entry.title: ITEM-017
entry.item_body: If sqrt(3x + 1) = 5, what is the value of x?

feedback.neutral: Square both sides to get 3x + 1 = 25. Then 3x = 24, so x = 8.

entry.title: ITEM-018
entry.item_body: For which values of x is the expression 1/(x^2 - 4x) undefined?

feedback.neutral: The denominator factors as x(x - 4). It is zero at x = 0 and x = 4.

answer_feedback.ITEM-018-A: This finds one zero of the denominator but misses x = 4.
answer_feedback.ITEM-018-B: This finds one zero of the denominator but misses x = 0.
answer_feedback.ITEM-018-D: This factors x^2 - 4x as if it were a difference of squares.

entry.title: ITEM-019
entry.item_body: If 4x - 9 = 2x + 7, what is the value of x?

feedback.neutral: Subtract 2x from both sides to get 2x - 9 = 7. Add 9 to get 2x = 16, so x = 8.

answer_feedback.ITEM-019-A: This divides the constant difference by the wrong coefficient.
answer_feedback.ITEM-019-B: This treats the right side as 7 and ignores the 2x term before solving.
answer_feedback.ITEM-019-D: This finds 2x but does not divide by 2.

entry.title: ITEM-020
entry.item_body: Which expression is equivalent to (2x^3)(3x^2)?

feedback.neutral: Multiply coefficients and add exponents on like bases: 2*3*x^(3+2) = 6x^5.

answer_feedback.ITEM-020-A: This adds the coefficients instead of multiplying them.
answer_feedback.ITEM-020-C: This multiplies the exponents instead of adding them.
answer_feedback.ITEM-020-D: This multiplies the coefficients by an extra factor of 2.

entry.title: ITEM-021
entry.item_body: A bag contains 5 red tiles, 3 blue tiles, and 2 green tiles. If one tile is selected at random, what is the probability that it is not blue?

feedback.neutral: There are 10 tiles total and 7 are not blue, so the probability is 7/10.

answer_feedback.ITEM-021-A: This is the probability of blue, not not blue.
answer_feedback.ITEM-021-C: This compares green tiles to non-blue tiles.
answer_feedback.ITEM-021-D: This reverses favorable and total counts.

entry.title: ITEM-022
entry.item_body: Two angles of a triangle measure 42 degrees and 68 degrees. What is the measure, in degrees, of the third angle?

feedback.neutral: The angles in a triangle sum to 180 degrees. The third angle is 180 - 42 - 68 = 70.

answer_feedback.ITEM-022-B: This assumes the triangle is right without evidence.
answer_feedback.ITEM-022-C: This adds the two given angles.
answer_feedback.ITEM-022-D: This subtracts one given angle from 180 but ignores the other.

entry.title: ITEM-023
entry.item_body: What is the y-intercept of the line 3x + 2y = 12?

feedback.neutral: Set x = 0 to find the y-intercept: 2y = 12, so y = 6.

answer_feedback.ITEM-023-A: This uses the coefficient of y, not the intercept.
answer_feedback.ITEM-023-B: This uses the coefficient of x, not the intercept.

entry.title: ITEM-024
entry.item_body: The function q(x) = (x - 4)^2 + 7 is graphed in the xy-plane. What is the vertex of the graph?

feedback.neutral: A quadratic in vertex form (x - h)^2 + k has vertex (h, k), so the vertex is (4, 7).

answer_feedback.ITEM-024-A: This uses the sign inside the parentheses without reversing it.
answer_feedback.ITEM-024-C: This switches the x- and y-coordinates.
answer_feedback.ITEM-024-D: This changes the sign of the vertical shift.

entry.title: ITEM-025
entry.item_body: A line is parallel to y = -3x + 2. What is the slope of the line?

feedback.neutral: Parallel lines have the same slope. The slope of y = -3x + 2 is -3.

answer_feedback.ITEM-025-B: This uses the negative reciprocal, which applies to perpendicular lines.
answer_feedback.ITEM-025-C: This uses the reciprocal and changes the sign.
answer_feedback.ITEM-025-D: This identifies the y-intercept of the given line.

entry.title: ITEM-026
entry.item_body: If 2^(x + 1) = 32, what is the value of x?

feedback.neutral: Since 32 = 2^5, x + 1 = 5 and x = 4.

entry.title: ITEM-027
entry.item_body: Two similar triangles have corresponding side lengths in the ratio 3:5. If the area of the smaller triangle is 36 square units, what is the area of the larger triangle?

feedback.neutral: Area scales by the square of the side scale factor. The larger area is 36*(5/3)^2 = 36*(25/9) = 100.

answer_feedback.ITEM-027-A: This scales area by the side-length ratio instead of the square of the ratio.
answer_feedback.ITEM-027-B: This treats the side scale factor as 2 instead of 5/3, then squares that incorrect factor.
answer_feedback.ITEM-027-D: This multiplies by 5 instead of using the squared scale factor.

entry.title: ITEM-028
entry.item_body: If 0.25x + 6 = 18, what is the value of x?

feedback.neutral: Subtract 6 to get 0.25x = 12. Since 0.25 = 1/4, x = 48.

entry.title: ITEM-029
entry.item_body: Which expression is equivalent to x^2 + 7x + 10?

feedback.neutral: Find two numbers that multiply to 10 and add to 7: 5 and 2. The factorization is (x + 5)(x + 2).

answer_feedback.ITEM-029-B: This gives the correct factors with incorrect signs.
answer_feedback.ITEM-029-C: This has the right product but the middle term would be 11x.
answer_feedback.ITEM-029-D: This uses the coefficient and constant as factors directly.

entry.title: ITEM-030
entry.item_body: Thirty percent of n is 45. What is the value of n?

feedback.neutral: Set 0.30n = 45. Dividing by 0.30 gives n = 150.

entry.title: ITEM-031
entry.item_body: A service charge is modeled by C = 25 + 8h, where C is the total cost in dollars for h hours. What does 8 represent in this model?

choices.item_body: The fixed cost before any hours are added

feedback.neutral: In C = 25 + 8h, the coefficient of h is the rate of change, so the cost is 8 dollars per hour.

answer_feedback.ITEM-031-A: The fixed cost is the constant 25.
answer_feedback.ITEM-031-B: The variable h represents the number of hours.
answer_feedback.ITEM-031-D: The coefficient 8 is a rate, not a total for 8 hours.

entry.title: ITEM-032
entry.item_body: What are the solutions to |x - 2| = 5?

feedback.neutral: The equation means x - 2 = 5 or x - 2 = -5, so x = 7 or x = -3.

answer_feedback.ITEM-032-B: This solves x - 2 = 5 but handles the negative case incorrectly.
answer_feedback.ITEM-032-C: This changes the sign of the wrong term.
answer_feedback.ITEM-032-D: This includes only the positive-distance solution.

entry.title: ITEM-033
entry.item_body: In a right triangle, sin(theta) = 3/5 and the hypotenuse has length 20. What is the length of the leg adjacent to theta?

feedback.neutral: If sin(theta) = 3/5, the opposite leg and hypotenuse are in the ratio 3:5. With hypotenuse 20, the scale factor is 4, so the opposite leg is 12. The adjacent leg is sqrt(20^2 - 12^2) = sqrt(256) = 16.

entry.title: ITEM-034
entry.item_body: Which inequality is equivalent to 2(x - 3) <= 10?

feedback.neutral: Distribute or divide by 2: x - 3 <= 5, so x <= 8.

answer_feedback.ITEM-034-A: This subtracts 6 from 10 after distributing instead of adding 6.
answer_feedback.ITEM-034-C: This reverses the inequality even though division is by a positive number.
answer_feedback.ITEM-034-D: This ignores the factor of 2 and solves x - 3 <= 10 before adding 3.

properties.subdomain: Linear inequalities in one or two variables

entry.title: ITEM-035
entry.item_body: If f(x) = x^2 + 2 and g(x) = 3x - 1, what is f(g(2))?

entry.title: ITEM-036
entry.item_body: If (x + 2)/(x - 1) = 3 and x is not equal to 1, what is the value of x?

feedback.neutral: Multiply by x - 1: x + 2 = 3x - 3. Then 5 = 2x, so x = 5/2.

answer_feedback.ITEM-036-A: This reverses the final division step and divides 2 by 5 instead of 5 by 2.
answer_feedback.ITEM-036-C: This changes the sign when moving terms after clearing the denominator.
answer_feedback.ITEM-036-D: This finds the numerator difference but does not divide by 2.
